opsNote: Vulcan dossiers work best as baseline Federation records: long planning horizons, tight protocol, and a homeworld that anchors half the diplomatic map.
Romulan records should always be read as political geography as much as culture: state secrecy, prestige, and border pressure all show up at once.
Klingon archives get more useful when you keep treaty history, house politics, and martial identity on the same screen.
Collective records are threat snapshots, not stable cultural profiles. Treat every Borg dossier as something that can reconfigure itself under pressure.

commonsQuery: United Federation of Planets Star Trek
opsNote: Federation records need ideals and logistics together: charter language means less unless you can also see the worlds, fleets, and treaties holding it up.

abstractOverride: Vulcan is the ancestral homeworld of the Vulcan people and one of the Federation's most important cultural and diplomatic anchor worlds.
opsNote: Use Vulcan as the archive's cleanest reference point for first-contact history, Federation founding culture, and Beta Quadrant core geography.

abstractOverride: Romulus served as the political heart of Romulan power, making it a key reference node for statecraft, secrecy, and diaspora after its loss.
opsNote: Romulus belongs on the map even when the dossier is about policy; nearly every Romulan strategic question bends back toward this world.

abstractOverride: Qo'noS is the Klingon homeworld and imperial center, useful as both a cultural anchor and a strategic map node inside the empire's core.
opsNote: Qo'noS should read as capital, symbol, and strategic pressure source at the same time; separating those layers makes Klingon records flatter than they are.
Spock is a bridge record. The interesting part is not the biography alone, but the way Vulcan, Starfleet, and Romulan lines cross through him.
Picard dossiers come alive when command history, diplomatic posture, and Borg exposure sit in the same visual field instead of separate tabs.
The Enterprise-D is less a ship card than a moving Federation stage for diplomacy, exploration, and crisis response across multiple sectors.

abstractOverride: The Khitomer Accords mark the diplomatic hinge that stabilized Federation-Klingon relations and reshaped the strategic order of the quadrant.
opsNote: This is a treaty record, but it should still feel geographic: Khitomer, Klingon politics, Federation response, and later alliance doctrine all converge here.
